category_title: Students distracted by personal circumstances and engaging in off-task talk during math instruction
scenario_title: Everyday numeracy lesson with off-task talk and personal distractions
minimal_context: The student and tutor are working through a worksheet on everyday numeracy applications displayed on an overhead projector.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Let's look at the first question on the overhead projector about calculating the weekly grocery budget.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: Why do we even have to do this? I find this curriculum irrelevant.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Please just try to write down the answer on your worksheet.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: I can't do it, I'm just not on the planet today. My whole experience of schooling is negative.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: We need to keep going with the lesson.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: (Calls out to another student across the room) Hey, did you see what happened this morning? I'm not doing this math, I'm not well, cross and cranky today. There's too much talking anyway!

dialogue_combined:
TUTOR: Let's look at the first question on the overhead projector about calculating the weekly grocery budget.

STUDENT: Why do we even have to do this? I find this curriculum irrelevant.

TUTOR: Please just try to write down the answer on your worksheet.

STUDENT: I can't do it, I'm just not on the planet today. My whole experience of schooling is negative.

TUTOR: We need to keep going with the lesson.

STUDENT: (Calls out to another student across the room) Hey, did you see what happened this morning? I'm not doing this math, I'm not well, cross and cranky today. There's too much talking anyway!

context.materials: Worksheet, tasks on an overhead projector slide, video clips
context.social_setting: Whole class working through questions using an initiation-response-evaluation format of teacher-led discussion

challenging_behavior_trigger.condition: During teacher-led discussion of mathematical problems
challenging_behavior_trigger.observable_behavior: Continually interrupted by topical diversions initiated by both students and teacher, excessive talking
challenging_behavior_trigger.internal_state: Not well, cross and cranky, not on the planet today

behavior_patterns.condition: When expected to focus on the worksheet or overhead projector tasks
behavior_patterns.observable_behavior: Engaging in excessive talking, calling out answers, continually flouting classroom expectations
behavior_patterns.internal_state: Experience of schooling is negative: they do not get on with teachers, they find the curriculum irrelevant or too hard, and they find the school environment unsupportive
